formulas.name: Unitary, Federal, and Confederation
formulas.formula: A system of government in which all power is held by a single central authority is called Unitary. A system of government in which power is divided between a central authority and constituent political units is called Federal. A system of government in which power is divided between a central authority and constituent political units, with the central authority having very little power is called Confederation.

formulas.name: Separation of Powers
formulas.formula: The separation of powers is a fundamental principle of governance, which divides power among three branches: the legislative, executive, and judicial.

formulas.name: Principle of Accountability
formulas.formula: The principle of accountability is a fundamental principle of governance, which holds public officials responsible for their actions and decisions.

formulas.name: Ministerial Responsibility
formulas.formula: Ministerial responsibility is a doctrine that holds ministers responsible for the actions and decisions of the executive.

formulas.name: Collective Responsibility
formulas.formula: Collective responsibility is a doctrine that holds the cabinet as a whole responsible for the actions and decisions of the executive.

formulas.name: Cabinet System
formulas.formula: The cabinet system is a type of executive system in which a group of ministers, led by the prime minister, advise the head of state and exercise executive power.

formulas.name: Council of Ministers
formulas.formula: The council of ministers is a group of ministers that advises the head of state and exercises executive power.

formulas.name: Office of the President
formulas.formula: The office of the president is the highest executive position in a country, which exercises executive power and serves as the head of state.

formulas.name: Office of the Prime Minister
formulas.formula: The office of the prime minister is the second-highest executive position in a country, which exercises executive power and advises the head of state.

formulas.name: Executive Authority
formulas.formula: Executive authority is the power exercised by the executive to make decisions and take actions on behalf of the state.

formulas.name: Executive Powers
formulas.formula: Executive powers are the authority and capacity of the executive to make decisions and take actions on behalf of the state.

formulas.name: Separation of Powers between Legislature and Executive
formulas.formula: The separation of powers between the legislature and executive refers to the division of power between the two branches, with the legislature responsible for making laws and the executive responsible for enforcing them.
